headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: SYNTHETIC CRAFT-PATTERN FRONT SWEEPS THE ETSY HOLLOWS AND PINTEREST SHALLOWS AS MACHINE-WRITTEN CROCHET INSTRUCTIONS DIRECT HOBBYISTS TO STITCH SHAPES THAT VIOLATE THE LAWS OF YARN
advisory: A dense band of fabricated craft patterns has settled over the maker regions, promising cozy amigurumi and heirloom afghans while issuing instructions that cannot be physically executed. Residents are advised that a pattern reading 'repeat rounds 4 through 9 until the piece forms a perfect sphere with no increases' is not a challenge to their skill but a defect in the system. The Service reminds crafters that genuine yardage totals were absent from these listings because no one, and nothing, ever picked up a hook. Purchase of the accompanying PDF is not recommended.

regional_outlook.conditions: Heavy pattern accumulation with widespread 'instant download' fog. Listings feature glossy hero images of finished objects that share no relationship with the twelve pages of instructions inside.
Persistent inspirational drizzle. Every board saturated with the same six impossible sweaters, each pinned nine thousand times and made by no one.
Scattered 'AI Pattern Generator' outbreaks moving in from the west, each promising unlimited free designs and delivering diagrams that resolve, on close inspection, into abstract anxiety.
Low visibility. Queries for 'easy beginner scarf' return blogs with 1,400 words of childhood reminiscence and a pattern that begins at row 3.
Lingering haze of 'Complete Crochet Masterclass' e-books, all 340 pages, all suspiciously free of any photograph of an actual completed stitch.

specimen.status: Abundant and multiplying; especially active during craft-season and gift-panic months.
specimen.habitat: Digital marketplace listings, hobby blogs, and pinboards, typically nesting behind a photorealistic image of a finished object it did not produce.
specimen.field_marks: Confident numbered rounds; total yardage requirements conspicuously missing or listed as 'as needed.' Watch for stitch counts that increase and decrease in the same round, gauge instructions measured in units that do not exist, and the phrase 'continue in pattern' where no pattern was ever established.
specimen.call: Round 7: sc in each st around, decreasing evenly until the fabric becomes seamless and the shape is intuitive. You've got this.
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for a legitimate independent designer's pattern, which also lives on these platforms and deserves your support. Tell them apart by the reviews: real patterns accumulate photos of lopsided but triumphant finished projects, while the synthetic variety collects only five-star ratings posted before download and one bewildered comment reading 'is round 12 possible?'

spotter_tip: Read the pattern aloud and try to actually knit it in your head. If at any point the yarn is instructed to pass through itself, form a Möbius scarf, or 'simply close the gap,' you are holding slop — put down your hook and back away.
tomorrow: Craft-pattern band lifts slightly; watch for an incoming surge of fake five-ingredient recipe blogs, each preceded by a 2,000-word memoir.
